---
title: "Técnicas Cuantitativas I"
subtitle: "Grado en ADE and Grado en Economía — Universidad de Granada"
---

::: {style="text-align: right; margin-bottom: 1em;"}
[← Back to Teaching](../teaching.html){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

A first-year undergraduate course covering descriptive statistics, probability theory, and an introduction to time series — the quantitative foundations students need before tackling econometrics. Hands-on labs in Excel and R.

## Topics

1. **[Univariate analysis](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/01-univariate.html).** Descriptive statistics for a single variable — central tendency, dispersion, shape; histograms and boxplots; data exploration in Excel and R.

2. **[Bivariate analysis](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/02-bivariate.html).** Joint behaviour of two variables — covariance, correlation, contingency tables; the simple linear regression as a descriptive tool.

3. **[Probability](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/03-probability.html).** Set-theoretic foundations and axioms; conditional probability and Bayes' theorem; independence; combinatorics.

4. **[Random variables](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/04-random-variables.html).** Discrete and continuous random variables; expectation, variance, moments; joint and conditional distributions.

5. **[Distributions](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/05-distributions.html).** Common probability models — Bernoulli, Binomial, Poisson, Normal, Student's $t$, $\chi^2$, $F$ — and their applications.

6. **[Index numbers](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/06-index-numbers.html).** Price and quantity indices (Laspeyres, Paasche, Fisher); inflation; chained vs fixed-base indices.

7. **[Time series](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/07-time-series.html).** Components (trend, seasonality, cycle, irregular); decomposition; moving averages; smoothing.

**Appendices** — [mathematical and statistical prerequisites](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/appendix-a-prerequisites.html) · [statistical tables](https://agaggero.github.io/quantitative-techniques-i/chapters/appendix-b-tables.html).

## Course logistics

- **Programme.** Grado en Economía · Grado en Administración y Dirección de Empresas
- **Year.** First-year undergraduate
- **Language of instruction.** English and Spanish (separate groups)
- **Software.** Microsoft Excel and R via RStudio
- **Assessment.** Continuous assessment (problem sets and Excel/R labs) plus a final exam.

## Materials

Lecture notes, beamer slides, problem sets with solutions, and per-topic Excel and R lab scripts.
